```{r plot21-trajectories, fig.width = 15, fig.height = 10}
    title    = "Individual binomial trajectories: model preference vs. corpus expectation over training",
    subtitle = "Largest model only  .  Blue = model preference  .  Orange = RelFreq expectation"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 6))
```

---

# Section E: Mixed-Effects Model and Variance Decomposition

How much variance in model preference is explained by relative frequency alone,
GenPref alone, both together, and a full model with interactions? The
mixed-effects version adds a random intercept per binomial to account for
the repeated-measures structure across checkpoints.

```{r vardecomp-setup, cache=TRUE, cache.lazy=FALSE}
library(lme4)

# ── Centre predictors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
vd_raw <- reg_data %>% filter(!is.na(genpref))

# genpref is already on [-0.5, 0.5] after normalisation in load-genpref.
# Centre by subtracting its mean (should be near 0 if binomials are balanced).
vd_data <- vd_raw %>%
  mutate(
    freq_prob_c = plogis(log_freq_ratio) - 0.5,
    genpref_c   = genpref - mean(genpref, na.rm = TRUE),
    log_total_c = log_total - mean(log_total, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat(sprintf("Predictors centred: genpref mean = %.4f  (range: [%.4f, %.4f])\n",
            mean(vd_raw$genpref, na.rm = TRUE),
            min(vd_raw$genpref,  na.rm = TRUE),
            max(vd_raw$genpref,  na.rm = TRUE)))

y      <- vd_data$mean_preference
ss_tot <- sum((y - mean(y))^2)

# ── Pre-compute fold assignments ONCE  -  shared by every model ─────────────────
# Folds are at the binomial level: ALL checkpoints for a binomial land in the
# same fold. Using identical folds for OLS and lmer makes Q^2 directly comparable.
K <- 5
set.seed(42)
unique_binoms <- unique(vd_data$binom)
fold_assign   <- setNames(
  sample(rep(1:K, length.out = length(unique_binoms))),
  unique_binoms
)

# ── CV function  -  5-fold binomial-grouped holdout ────────────────────────────
lobo_q2 <- function(formula, data, fit_fn) {
  binom_vals <- data$binom
  lobo_ss    <- 0
  for (fold in seq_len(K)) {
    held     <- names(fold_assign)[fold_assign == fold]
    in_train <- !binom_vals %in% held
    fit_k    <- fit_fn(formula, data[in_train, ])
    pred     <- predict(fit_k,
                        newdata          = data[!in_train, ],
                        allow.new.levels = TRUE)
    lobo_ss  <- lobo_ss + sum((data$mean_preference[!in_train] - pred)^2)
  }
  1 - lobo_ss / ss_tot
}

lmer_fn <- function(frm, dat) lmer(frm, data = dat, REML = FALSE,
                                   control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))

# ── Model specifications (mixed-effects only) ─────────────────────────────────
mixed_specs <- list(
  "Null (intercept only)"                    = mean_preference ~ 1                                                              + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency"                                = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c                                                    + (1 | binom),
  "GenPref"                                  = mean_preference ~ genpref_c                                                      + (1 | binom),
  "Overall frequency"                        = mean_preference ~ log_total_c                                                  + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency + GenPref"                      = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c + genpref_c                                        + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency + Overall"                      = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c + log_total_c                                    + (1 | binom),
  "GenPref + Overall"                        = mean_preference ~ genpref_c + log_total_c                                      + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency + GenPref + Overall"            = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c + genpref_c + log_total_c                        + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency x Overall"                      = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c * log_total_c                                    + (1 | binom),
  "GenPref x Overall"                        = mean_preference ~ genpref_c * log_total_c                                      + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency x Overall + GenPref"            = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c * log_total_c + genpref_c                        + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency + GenPref x Overall"            = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c + genpref_c * log_total_c                        + (1 | binom),
  "Frequency x Overall + GenPref x Overall"  = mean_preference ~ freq_prob_c * log_total_c + genpref_c * log_total_c        + (1 | binom)
)

# ── Run 5-fold binomial-grouped LOO-CV for every mixed model ──────────────────
cat(sprintf("Running %d-fold binomial-grouped CV for %d mixed models...\n",
            K, length(mixed_specs)))

cv_table <- purrr::imap_dfr(mixed_specs, function(frm, name) {
  cat(sprintf("  Mixed: %s\n", name))
  q2_mix  <- tryCatch(lobo_q2(frm, vd_data, lmer_fn),
                      error = function(e) { warning(e); NA_real_ })
  fit_mix <- tryCatch(lmer_fn(frm, vd_data), error = function(e) NULL)

  tibble(
    Model    = name,
    LOBO_Q2  = round(q2_mix, 4),
    AIC      = if (!is.null(fit_mix)) round(AIC(fit_mix), 1) else NA_real_,
    n_params = if (!is.null(fit_mix)) length(fixef(fit_mix)) + 2L else NA_integer_
  )
})

print(cv_table %>% select(Model, LOBO_Q2, AIC, n_params))
```

```{r vardecomp-summary}
# ── 1. Ranked table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("=== Models ranked by LOBO-CV Q^2 (best first) ===\n")
cv_ranked <- cv_table %>%
  arrange(desc(LOBO_Q2)) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number(),
         delta_Q2_from_null = round(LOBO_Q2 - LOBO_Q2[Model == "Null (intercept only)"], 4))
print(cv_ranked %>% select(rank, Model, LOBO_Q2, delta_Q2_from_null, AIC, n_params))

# ── 2. Marginal contribution of each component ────────────────────────────────
# Compare pairs of nested models that differ by exactly one component
null_q2  <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Null (intercept only)"]
freq_q2  <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Frequency"]
gp_q2    <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "GenPref"]
ovr_q2   <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Overall frequency"]
fgp_q2   <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Frequency + GenPref"]
fovr_q2  <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Frequency + Overall"]
all3_q2  <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Frequency + GenPref + Overall"]
fxovr_q2 <- cv_table$LOBO_Q2[cv_table$Model == "Frequency x Overall + GenPref x Overall"]

marginal <- tribble(
  ~Comparison,                                          ~delta_Q2,
  "Null -> Frequency (main effect)",                    round(freq_q2  - null_q2, 4),
  "Null -> GenPref (main effect)",                      round(gp_q2    - null_q2, 4),
  "Null -> Overall frequency (main effect)",            round(ovr_q2   - null_q2, 4),
  "Frequency -> Frequency + GenPref",                   round(fgp_q2   - freq_q2, 4),
  "Frequency -> Frequency + Overall",                   round(fovr_q2  - freq_q2, 4),
  "Freq + GenPref + Overall -> full interactions",      round(fxovr_q2 - all3_q2, 4)
)
cat("\n=== Marginal Q^2 gain from adding each component ===\n")
print(marginal)

# ── 3. Best model: full fixed-effect summary ──────────────────────────────────
best_name <- cv_ranked$Model[1]
best_frm  <- mixed_specs[[best_name]]
cat(sprintf("\n=== Best model: '%s'  (Q^2 = %.4f) ===\n", best_name,
            cv_ranked$LOBO_Q2[1]))
best_fit <- lmer(best_frm, data = vd_data, REML = FALSE,
                 control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
print(summary(best_fit), correlation = FALSE)

cat("\n=== Random-effects variance decomposition ===\n")
vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(best_fit))
vc$pct <- round(100 * vc$vcov / sum(vc$vcov), 1)
print(vc %>% select(grp, var1, vcov, pct))
```

```{r vardecomp-plot, fig.width = 11, fig.height = 7}
cv_table %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = rev(Model))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = LOBO_Q2, y = Model)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, fill = "#2ca02c") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", LOBO_Q2)), hjust = -0.1, size = 2.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "LOBO-CV Q^2  (leave-one-binomial-out predictive R^2)",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.2))
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL) +
  labs(
    title    = "Out-of-sample predictive accuracy  -  mixed-effects models",
    subtitle = paste("5-fold binomial-grouped CV  .",
                     "Holdout unit = entire binomial (all checkpoints)  .",
                     "Freq = P(alpha) - 0.5  .  GenPref on [-0.5, 0.5]  .",
                     "All models include (1|binom)")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
```

---

# Section F: What Determines the Direction of the Signed Gap?

When the model diverges from relative frequency, is that divergence explained by
GenPref, overall frequency, corpus-predicted frequency, or interactions among
these? Here `signed_gap` (`mean_preference - log_freq_ratio`) is the explicit
outcome, so every coefficient directly describes a predictor of the gap rather
than of raw preference.

A positive signed gap means the model is *more* alpha-preferring than the
corpus predicts; a negative gap means *less*.

```{r gap-direction-setup, cache=TRUE, cache.lazy=FALSE}
# Re-use centred predictors from vd_data (same centering as Section E)
gd_data <- vd_data %>%
  mutate(
    signed_gap   = mean_preference - log_freq_ratio,
    gap_positive = as.integer(signed_gap > 0)
  )

cat(sprintf("Gap-direction data: %d observations, %d unique binomials\n",
            nrow(gd_data), n_distinct(gd_data$binom)))

# ── Final-checkpoint subset (feasible for brms) ────────────────────────────────
gd_final <- gd_data %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size, model) %>%
  filter(tokens == max(tokens)) %>%
  ungroup()

cat(sprintf("Final-checkpoint subset: %d observations, %d unique binomials\n",
            nrow(gd_final), n_distinct(gd_final$binom)))

# ── Bayesian linear mixed model: signed gap ~ predictors + (1|binom) ──────────
# freq_prob_c  = P(alpha in corpus) - 0.5  (what corpus predicts, centred)
# genpref_c    = GenPref - mean            (linguistic preference, centred)
# log_total_c  = log(total_seen) - mean    (overall exposure, centred)
gd_fit <- brm(
  signed_gap ~ genpref_c * log_total_c + freq_prob_c * log_total_c + (1 | binom),
  data   = gd_final,
  family = gaussian(),
  prior  = BRMS_PRIORS,
  iter   = BRMS_ITER,
  warmup = BRMS_WARMUP,
  chains = BRMS_CHAINS,
  cores  = BRMS_CORES,
  file   = file.path(MODELS_DIR, "gd_fit"),
  seed   = BRMS_SEED
)
cat("\n=== Bayesian linear mixed model (final checkpoint): signed_gap ===\n")
print(summary(gd_fit))

# ── Bayesian logistic mixed model: P(gap > 0) ─────────────────────────────────
gd_logit <- brm(
  gap_positive ~ genpref_c * log_total_c + freq_prob_c * log_total_c + (1 | binom),
  data   = gd_final,
  family = bernoulli(),
  prior  = BRMS_PRIORS_LOGISTIC,
  iter   = BRMS_ITER,
  warmup = BRMS_WARMUP,
  chains = BRMS_CHAINS,
  cores  = BRMS_CORES,
  file   = file.path(MODELS_DIR, "gd_logit"),
  seed   = BRMS_SEED + 1L
)
cat("\n=== Bayesian logistic mixed model (final checkpoint): P(gap > 0) ===\n")
print(summary(gd_logit))

# ── Coefficient table for plotting ────────────────────────────────────────────
extract_coefs_brms <- function(fit, model_label) {
  fe        <- as.data.frame(fixef(fit))
  fe$term   <- rownames(fe)
  fe$model  <- model_label
  fe
}

TERM_LEVELS_GD <- c("freq_prob_c", "genpref_c", "log_total_c",
                    "freq_prob_c:log_total_c", "genpref_c:log_total_c")
TERM_LABELS_GD <- c("RelFreq\n(P(alpha)-0.5)", "GenPref",
                    "Overall freq\n(ln total)",
                    "RelFreq x\nOverall freq", "GenPref x\nOverall freq")

gd_coefs <- bind_rows(
  extract_coefs_brms(gd_fit,   "Linear (signed gap)"),
  extract_coefs_brms(gd_logit, "Logistic (gap > 0)")
) %>%
  rename(estimate = Estimate, se = Est.Error, lo = Q2.5, hi = Q97.5) %>%
  filter(term != "Intercept") %>%
  mutate(
    term = factor(term, levels = TERM_LEVELS_GD, labels = TERM_LABELS_GD),
    # Mark coefficients whose 95% credible interval excludes zero
    sig  = if_else(lo > 0 | hi < 0, "*", "")
  )
```

```{r gap-direction-plot, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 5}
ggplot(gd_coefs,
       aes(x = term, y = estimate,
           colour = model, group = model)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.5), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(0.5)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig, y = hi + 0.02),
            position = position_dodge(0.5), size = 4, vjust = 0) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Linear (signed gap)" = "#1f77b4",
                                 "Logistic (gap > 0)"  = "#ff7f0e"),
                      name = NULL) +
  scale_x_discrete(name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Coefficient  (95% credible interval)") +
  facet_wrap(~model, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title    = "What predicts the direction and magnitude of the signed gap?",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Linear model outcome: signed_gap = mean_preference - log_freq_ratio  -  ",
      "Logistic outcome: P(gap > 0)\n",
      "All predictors centred  -  Random intercept by binomial  -  Final checkpoint  -  ",
      "Bayesian (brms)  -  * = 95% CI excludes zero")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9))
```

```{r gap-direction-genpref-plot, fig.width = 11, fig.height = 5}
# Visualise the GenPref effect directly: mean signed gap binned by GenPref decile
genpref_bins <- gd_data %>%
  mutate(genpref_decile = ntile(genpref_c, 10)) %>%
  group_by(corpus, genpref_decile) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_genpref  = mean(genpref_c + mean(gd_data$genpref, na.rm = TRUE),
                         na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_gap      = mean(signed_gap,      na.rm = TRUE),
    se_gap        = sd(signed_gap, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    pct_positive  = mean(signed_gap > 0,  na.rm = TRUE),
    n             = n(),
    .groups       = "drop"
  )

ggplot(genpref_bins,
       aes(x = mean_genpref, y = mean_gap, colour = corpus)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_gap - 1.96 * se_gap,
                    ymax = mean_gap + 1.96 * se_gap),
                width = 0.01, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("BabyLM" = "#1b7837", "C4" = "#762a83"),
                      name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "GenPref  (binned by decile, original scale)") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean signed gap  (log-odds)") +
  facet_wrap(~corpus) +
  labs(
    title    = "Mean signed gap by GenPref decile",
    subtitle = "Positive gap = model more alpha-preferring than corpus predicts  -  All checkpoints & model sizes pooled  -  +/-95% CI"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

---

# Section G: Cross-Model-Size Consistency of Deviation

If the deviation from frequency expectation is structural (driven by
properties of the binomial such as GenPref), the direction and magnitude of
deviation should be consistent across model sizes.

```{r consistency-setup, cache=TRUE}
# Long format: one row per corpus x binom x model_size at each model's final checkpoint.
# Average across models of the same size within a corpus (handles multiple seeds/runs).
consistency_long <- agg_gp %>%
  filter(!is.na(log_freq_ratio)) %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size, binom) %>%
  filter(tokens == max(tokens)) %>%
  summarise(signed_gap = mean(mean_preference - log_freq_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(model_size = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS))

cat(sprintf("Consistency data: %d rows, %d unique binomials, %d model sizes\n",
            nrow(consistency_long),
            n_distinct(consistency_long$binom),
            n_distinct(consistency_long$model_size)))
```

```{r plot22-consistency, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 5}
size_pairs <- combn(SIZE_LEVELS, 2, simplify = FALSE)

for (pair in size_pairs) {
  # Inner join: only binomials present in BOTH sizes (avoids NA from pivot_wider)
  d <- inner_join(
    consistency_long %>%
      filter(model_size == pair[1]) %>%
      select(corpus, binom, gap_x = signed_gap),
    consistency_long %>%
      filter(model_size == pair[2]) %>%
      select(corpus, binom, gap_y = signed_gap),
    by = c("corpus", "binom")
  )

  if (nrow(d) == 0) next

  r_vals <- d %>%
    group_by(corpus) %>%
    summarise(r    = round(cor(gap_x, gap_y, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
              n    = n(),
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(label = paste0("r = ", r, "  (n = ", scales::comma(n), ")"))

  p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = gap_x, y = gap_y)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.06, size = 0.5, colour = "grey40") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
                linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "#d62728", linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_text(data = r_vals, aes(label = label),
              x = -Inf, y = Inf, hjust = -0.08, vjust = 1.4,
              size = 3, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(name = paste(pair[1], "deviation (log-odds)")) +
    scale_y_continuous(name = paste(pair[2], "deviation (log-odds)")) +
    facet_wrap(~corpus) +
    labs(title    = paste("Deviation consistency:", pair[1], "vs.", pair[2]),
         subtitle = "Only binomials present in both model sizes  .  Dashed = perfect agreement  .  Final checkpoint") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 8))

  print(p)
}
```

---

# Section H: Outlier Characterization

Which binomials show the largest persistent deviation from relative frequency
across all checkpoints? These are the clearest cases where the model is not
simply copying the corpus.

```{r outliers-setup, cache=TRUE}
outliers <- agg_gp %>%
  filter(!is.na(log_freq_ratio), !is.na(genpref)) %>%
  mutate(signed_gap = mean_preference - log_freq_ratio) %>%
  group_by(corpus, binom) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_signed_gap  = mean(signed_gap,    na.rm = TRUE),
    abs_mean_gap     = abs(mean(signed_gap, na.rm = TRUE)),
    sd_gap           = sd(signed_gap,      na.rm = TRUE),
    genpref          = first(na.omit(genpref)),
    final_total_seen = total_seen[which.max(tokens)],
    n_checkpoints    = n(),
    .groups          = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(corpus) %>%
  slice_max(abs_mean_gap, n = 20) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(corpus, desc(abs_mean_gap))

print(outliers %>% select(corpus, binom, mean_signed_gap, genpref,
                          final_total_seen, n_checkpoints))
```

```{r plot23-outliers, fig.width = 13, fig.height = 7}
outliers_plot <- outliers %>%
  group_by(corpus) %>%
  mutate(binom_f = factor(binom, levels = binom[order(mean_signed_gap)])) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(outliers_plot,
       aes(x = mean_signed_gap, y = binom_f, fill = genpref)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1,
                       name = "GenPref", limits = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Mean signed gap  (preference - log_freq_ratio, log-odds)"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~corpus, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 20 binomials with largest persistent deviation from frequency expectation",
    subtitle = "Fill = GenPref  .  Right = model more alpha-preferring than corpus predicts  .  Left = less"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position  = "right",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
        strip.text         = element_text(size = 9),
        axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 7))
```

---

# Summary Statistics: Over- vs. Under-Prediction Relative to Corpus Frequency

**Positive signed gap** (`mean_preference > log_freq_ratio`) means the model
prefers the alpha order *more* than relative frequency predicts  -  it is
over-predicting the alpha order.

**Negative signed gap** means the model is less alpha-biased than the corpus  - 
under-predicting the alpha order.

```{r overpred-setup, cache=TRUE}
# ── All checkpoints pooled ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
gap_base <- agg_gp %>%
  filter(!is.na(log_freq_ratio), !is.na(total_seen), total_seen > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    signed_gap  = mean_preference - log_freq_ratio,
    log_total = log(total_seen),
    freq_bin    = cut(log_total,
                      breaks = c(0, log(10), log(100), log(1000), Inf),
                      labels = c("1-10", "10-100", "100-1k", ">1k"),
                      include.lowest = TRUE),
    model_size  = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS)
  )

# ── Overall summary: mean signed gap per corpus x model_size ─────────────────
overall_summary <- gap_base %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gap   = mean(signed_gap, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_gap = median(signed_gap, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_gap     = sd(signed_gap, na.rm = TRUE),
    n          = n(),
    se         = sd_gap / sqrt(n),
    t_stat     = mean_gap / se,
    p_value    = 2 * pt(-abs(t_stat), df = n - 1),
    direction  = if_else(mean_gap > 0, "Over-predicts alpha order", "Under-predicts alpha order"),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean_gap, median_gap, sd_gap, se), ~round(.x, 4)),
         p_value = signif(p_value, 3))

cat("=== Overall over/under-prediction (signed, all checkpoints pooled) ===\n")
print(overall_summary %>%
        select(corpus, model_size, direction, mean_gap, median_gap, se, p_value, n))

# ── Overall summary: mean ABSOLUTE gap ───────────────────────────────────────
# The signed mean can cancel if some binomials are over- and others under-
# predicted. The mean absolute gap avoids this.
abs_overall_summary <- gap_base %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_abs_gap   = mean(abs(signed_gap),   na.rm = TRUE),
    median_abs_gap = median(abs(signed_gap), na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_abs_gap     = sd(abs(signed_gap),     na.rm = TRUE),
    n              = n(),
    se             = sd_abs_gap / sqrt(n),
    # One-sample t-test: is mean |gap| significantly greater than 0?
    t_stat         = mean_abs_gap / se,
    p_value        = pt(-abs(t_stat), df = n - 1, lower.tail = TRUE),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean_abs_gap, median_abs_gap, sd_abs_gap, se), ~round(.x, 4)),
         p_value    = signif(p_value, 3),
         model_size = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS))

cat("\n=== Overall absolute deviation from frequency expectation ===\n")
cat("(Mean |preference - log_freq_ratio|  -  not affected by cancellation)\n")
print(abs_overall_summary %>%
        select(corpus, model_size, mean_abs_gap, median_abs_gap, se, p_value, n))

# ── By frequency bin (signed and absolute) ───────────────────────────────────
freq_summary <- gap_base %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size, freq_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gap      = mean(signed_gap,      na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_abs_gap  = mean(abs(signed_gap), na.rm = TRUE),
    se_signed     = sd(signed_gap,        na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    se_abs        = sd(abs(signed_gap),   na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n             = n(),
    pct_over      = mean(signed_gap > 0,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean_gap, mean_abs_gap, se_signed, se_abs), ~round(.x, 4)),
         pct_over   = round(pct_over, 3),
         model_size = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS))

cat("\n=== Signed and absolute deviation by overall frequency bin ===\n")
print(freq_summary)
```

```{r overpred-overall-plot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4}
# Signed gap  -  direction of bias
ggplot(overall_summary,
       aes(x = model_size, y = mean_gap,
           colour = mean_gap > 0, group = 1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_gap - 1.96 * se,
                    ymax = mean_gap + 1.96 * se),
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#d62728", "FALSE" = "#1f77b4"),
                      labels = c("TRUE" = "Over-predicts", "FALSE" = "Under-predicts"),
                      name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean signed gap  (preference - log_freq_ratio)") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Model size") +
  facet_wrap(~corpus) +
  labs(title    = "Direction of bias: does the model over- or under-predict the alpha order?",
       subtitle = "All checkpoints pooled  .  Error bars = 95% CI") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r overpred-abs-overall-plot, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4}
# Absolute gap  -  magnitude of deviation regardless of direction
ggplot(abs_overall_summary,
       aes(x = model_size, y = mean_abs_gap,
           colour = model_size, group = 1)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abs_gap - 1.96 * se,
                    ymax = mean_abs_gap + 1.96 * se),
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean |preference - log_freq_ratio|",
                     limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Model size") +
  facet_wrap(~corpus) +
  labs(
    title    = "Magnitude of deviation from frequency expectation (absolute gap)",
    subtitle = "Not affected by cancellation of opposite errors  .  All checkpoints pooled  .  Error bars = 95% CI"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r overpred-byfreq-plot, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 9}
# Side-by-side: signed and absolute gap by frequency bin
freq_long <- freq_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(cols      = c(mean_gap, mean_abs_gap),
               names_to  = "metric",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    se_val = if_else(metric == "mean_gap", se_signed, se_abs),
    metric = factor(metric,
                    levels = c("mean_gap", "mean_abs_gap"),
                    labels = c("Signed gap\n(cancellation possible)",
                               "Absolute gap\n(no cancellation)"))
  )

ggplot(freq_long,
       aes(x = freq_bin, y = value,
           colour = model_size, group = model_size)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = value - 1.96 * se_val,
                    ymax = value + 1.96 * se_val),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.4), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  geom_point(size = 3,   position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Times binomial seen in corpus  (total alpha + beta)") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Gap (log-odds)") +
  facet_grid(metric ~ corpus, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title    = "Signed vs. absolute deviation from frequency expectation by exposure level",
    subtitle = "All checkpoints pooled  .  Error bars = 95% CI"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(size = 9, angle = 0, hjust = 0))
```

## Reconciling the Frequency-Bin Plot with the Scatter Plot

The frequency-bin plot uses four bins (1-10, 10-100, 100-1k, **>1k**), where the
final bin collapses `total_seen` from 1,000 to 100,000+ into a single average.
If the gap peaks somewhere in the 1k-10k range and then declines at very high
exposure (as the continuous LOESS suggests), the ">1k" bin average will be
pulled downward by the dense right tail, making it appear smaller than the
"100-1k" bin  -  producing an apparent contradiction.

The plot below breaks the ">1k" bin into finer sub-bins to reveal whether the
relationship is non-monotone at high frequency.

```{r overpred-finebins, cache=TRUE, fig.width = 13, fig.height = 9}
fine_summary <- gap_base %>%
  mutate(
    freq_bin_fine = cut(
      log_total,
      breaks = c(0, log(10), log(100), log(1000), log(3000), log(10000), log(30000), log(100000), Inf),
      labels = c("1-10", "10-100", "100-1k",
                 "1k-3k", "3k-10k", "10k-30k", "30k-100k", ">100k"),
      include.lowest = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(corpus, model_size, freq_bin_fine) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_abs_gap = mean(abs(signed_gap), na.rm = TRUE),
    se_abs       = sd(abs(signed_gap),   na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n            = n(),
    .groups      = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(model_size = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS))

ggplot(fine_summary,
       aes(x = freq_bin_fine, y = mean_abs_gap,
           colour = model_size, group = model_size)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abs_gap - 1.96 * se_abs,
                    ymax = mean_abs_gap + 1.96 * se_abs),
                width = 0.3, position = position_dodge(0.5), linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, position = position_dodge(0.5)) +
  geom_point(size = 3,    position = position_dodge(0.5)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Times binomial seen in corpus  (total alpha + beta)") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "|preference - log_freq_ratio|  (log-odds)",
                     limits = c(0, NA)) +
  facet_wrap(~corpus) +
  labs(
    title    = "Absolute gap by fine-grained frequency bin   -   reconciling bin plot vs. scatter LOESS",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "The coarse '>1k' bin hid the high-frequency tail. If gap peaks at 1k-10k\n",
      "then declines, the '>1k' average is pulled down  -  explaining the apparent contradiction.\n",
      "All checkpoints pooled  -  Error bars = 95% CI")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r overpred-pct-plot, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 6}
# What proportion of binomials are over-predicted at each frequency level?
ggplot(freq_summary,
       aes(x = freq_bin, y = pct_over,
           colour = model_size, group = model_size)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  geom_point(size = 3,   position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Times binomial seen in corpus  (total alpha + beta)") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Proportion of binomials over-predicted",
                     labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~corpus) +
  labs(
    title    = "Proportion of binomials where model is more alpha-preferring than relative frequency predicts",
    subtitle = "Dashed line = 50% (no systematic bias)  .  All checkpoints pooled"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```
